Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait, is there a prescription for this patient? Event 14 is prescription for 62F. The 45M patient gets discharged without an explicit prescription event shown. Thats plausible - maybe no prescription needed, or followup at outpatient clinic handles it.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait a minute. Let me look again. Is Event 6 actually for the surgery patient? Its in Surgery department, 28M, reason=scheduled_knee_surgery. Yes. Now, Event 12: Doctor consultation, Dr. Lee, ER, 45, M, diagnosis=hairline_fracture. Dr. Lee also appears in Events 6 and 13 (Surgery). Thats fine - doctors can work across departments or the same doctor ID could be in different departments

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The patient attributes clearly delineate three cases based on age+gender combinations: - 45, M: 5 events (1, 4, 7, 12, 15) - 62, F: 7 events (2, 5, 8, 10, 11, 14, 17) - 28, M: 6 events (3, 6, 9, 13, 16, 18) Every event has a unique combination of age and gender that matches exactly one of these three profiles. Since there are no two patients with the same age and gender, the case grouping is straightforward.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Sequence for 45M: Register Triage X-ray Doctor consultation Discharge. This makes sense for an ER fracture case. Sequence for 62F: Register Triage Lab request Lab completed Doctor consultation Prescription Discharge. This makes sense for an ER infection case. Sequence for 28M: Register Doctor consultation (scheduled) Surgery prep Surgery Recovery Discharge. This makes sense for a scheduled surgery. Everything interleaves properly by time. The fact that timestamps are strictly increasing and follow logical process orders for each case confirms the groupings.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me check if there are edge cases. Could Event 12 (Dr. Lee, ER, 45M) actually belong with the 28M surgery case? No, because patient_age and patient_gender explicitly differ. One small detail: Event 6 for the 28M is in the Surgery department and happens before surgery prep and surgery. Since its reason=scheduled_knee_surgery, this is likely a pre-op consultation. Another detail: Event 12 (45M) has Dr. Lee in the ER. But Dr. Lee is also the surgeon in Event 13 (28M). This is fine if Dr. Lee is an orthopedic specialist consulted in the ER for the fracture. So the three cases are solid.
